titleStmt.title: Letter from Richard Perceval Maxwell to Colonel Robert David Perceval Maxwell, 25 April, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Richard Perceval Maxwell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Lieutenant Richard Perceval-Maxwell (1897-1918), to his father, Colonel Robert David Perceval Maxwell (1870-1932), informing him that he has rejoined his battalion behind the front lines in a coal mining town and that he will look out for him. Lieutenant Richard Henry Perceval-Maxwell served in the Cameronians (Scottish Rifles) during the Great War, along with his father and brother. Richard was killed in action in July 1918. Colonel Robert Maxwell was wounded twice while serving with the 36th (Ulster) Division during the First World War.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland
msIdentifier.collection: Perceval-Maxwell Papers D/1556/27/3/2/1, Perceval-Maxwell Papers D/1556/27/3/2/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10th Scottish Rifles B.E.F. France April 25th. Dear Father, I have joined the 10th
Batt, Scottish Rifles again and
am in the same Coy as before.
We are at present a good
way behind the line in a
village, inwith a lovely bed
and baths fairly handy. We are in a mining district
so perhaps you can guess where
abouts we are. Jock's Battalion
is around here but is at
present in the line, however,
I hope to meet him some
time. I am going down a
coal mine tomorrow if possible
it ought to be rather interesting.
I hope you are out at rest
now for you've had a rotten
time. I don't think there's any
more news. I am in the 15th
Division same as before. So
look out for me and I will
do the same. Best of luck Yrs Dick
